title: Kotlin Flow
content:
### Flow in Kotlin

Flow is a cold asynchronous data stream that emits values sequentially. It is part of `kotlinx.coroutines.flow`.

---

### Creating Flows

```kotlin
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.*

// Flow builder
val numberFlow = flow {
    for (i in 1..5) {
        delay(500)
        emit(i)
    }
}

// From fixed values
val fixed = flowOf(1, 2, 3)

// From collections
val fromList = listOf(1, 2, 3).asFlow()
```

---

### Collecting Flows

```kotlin
fun main() = runBlocking {
    val flow = flowOf("A", "B", "C")
    
    flow.collect { value ->
        println(value)
    }
    
    // Or: flow.collect { println(it) }
}
```

---

### Flow Characteristics

| Characteristic | Description |
|---------------|-------------|
| Cold stream | Starts emitting only when collected |
| Sequential | Values emitted one by one |
| Cancellable | Cooperative cancellation |
| Structured | Respects coroutine scope |

---

### Flow Operators

```kotlin
flowOf(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
    .filter { it % 2 == 0 }
    .map { it * 10 }
    .take(2)
    .collect { println(it) }  // 20, 40
```

---

### Terminal Operators

| Operator | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `collect` | Collects all values |
| `first()` | Gets first value |
| `single()` | Expects exactly one value |
| `toList()` | Collects to list |
| `count()` | Counts values |
| `reduce()` | Accumulates values |

---

### Flow Exception Handling

```kotlin
fun main() = runBlocking {
    flow {
        emit(1)
        emit(2)
        throw RuntimeException("Error!")
    }
    .catch { e ->
        println("Caught: ${e.message}")
        emit(-1)  // Emit fallback value
    }
    .collect { println(it) }
}
// Output: 1, 2, Caught: Error!, -1
```

---

### Flow Completion

```kotlin
fun main() = runBlocking {
    flowOf("A", "B", "C")
        .onCompletion { cause ->
            if (cause == null)
                println("Completed successfully")
            else
                println("Failed: ${cause.message}")
        }
        .collect { println(it) }
}
```

> **Key Point:** Flow is a cold, asynchronous stream. Each `collect` triggers a new stream emission. Use `catch` for error recovery and `onCompletion` for cleanup.
